name: Norfolk Island (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the green flanking stripes representing the lush vegetation of Norfolk Island
the central panel bearing the territorial emblem

description: A vertical tricolour of green, white and green (with a wider central white panel) charged with a green Norfolk Island pine in the centre.
history: Adopted on 17 January 1980 by an Act of the Norfolk Island Legislative Assembly, after Norfolk Island achieved limited self-government within Australia in 1979. The design — a vertical tricolour with a central emblem — closely echoes the flag of Canada (1965), substituting the locally distinctive Norfolk Island pine for the maple leaf. Norfolk Island is an external territory of Australia.

features.symbolism: green stripes flanking a wider central white panel bearing a Norfolk Island pine — modelled after the flag of Canada, with the local emblem in place of the maple leaf
the Norfolk Island pine is endemic to the island and is its most distinctive natural emblem
